note: Renamed appendRetryDecisionBestEffort to appendRetryDecision; it now returns error. In the retry loop a new retryArtifactErr variable captures the return value; if non-nil the loop breaks and the error is propagated before any further lifecycle work, so a retry can no longer proceed without a durable JSONL record.

Added hasAuthPhrase field to textSignals (populated by reAuth phrase-only matching, distinct from the code-based re401/re403 signals). In classifyACPInternalError, hasAuthPhrase is checked before has5xx so that a genuine outer auth-phrase failure (e.g. 'Authentication required: gateway returned 500') is classified permanent instead of retryable. The code-only check (hasAuth, covering bare 401/403) remains after has5xx so that the existing 'outer 503 with incidental 403' test case continues to return server_error. A new table entry 'ACP -32603 auth phrase with incidental 5xx' exercises and pins the fixed behavior.

Added Jitter func() float64 to App and a jitter() helper method that returns a uniform random factor in [0.5, 1.5] when Jitter is nil. transportRetryDelay now accepts a jitter float64 parameter and multiplies the exponential base delay by it. All retry tests set app.Jitter = func() float64 { return 1.0 } so delay_ms records remain deterministic; the two tests asserting exact 1000ms/2000ms values continue to pass.

Added completedDespiteTimeoutReadOnlyTransport and TestCompletedDespiteTimeoutSuppressesRetry. The test runs a contract draft against a transport that returns CompletedDespiteTimeout=true with valid output, verifies the transport is called exactly once, and asserts that no retry-decisions.jsonl artifact is written.

TestReadOnlyIdleTimeoutRetryable now passes context.Canceled (the real sentinel) as the transport error alongside timedOut=true; the lifecycle's idle-timeout override promotes it to retryable. TestReadOnlyContextCanceledNotTimedOutNoRetry likewise passes context.Canceled with timedOut=false; ClassifyTransportError returns Kind='canceled' Retryable=false and no retry is scheduled.

Same fix as f_001: appendRetryDecision now returns error for all call sites including the final no-retry record on the last call. Any write failure is propagated out of the lifecycle rather than silently dropped.
